author: @michaeljames5346
text: $2300-2800/ per ton of graphite.  A typical quarry charges around $50 per ton for gravel. Looks like a solid endeavor to blast mine and get some equipment in there.

author: @DavidBelliveau
text: Hi Robert, How many kWh of storage do you think you can get with a tonne of graphite?

author: @jimsmindonline
text:
What ever happened to the mine?
Been checking out some of your old videos recently, this must have been quite an adventure!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it was mate and it is still there - they are having a few problems but i hear they are gradually getting out of them - i went to see it last year

author: @jasonflight3768
text: Newfoundland Canada, it is quite different from what you see in Sri Lanka. Graphite is on a regional scale and forms a schist 100m thick and ~2.2km in strike.  Flake graphite is common, but extraction seems to be a challenge.  At this stage the copper and gold on the property seem to be the more viable commodities, but I am still studying the graphite as such a large resource is very difficult to ignore.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Jason Flight I wouldn't mind visiting sometime mate - i keep an active interest in the field and you are right a sizeable deposit if graphite is very interesting especially to me lol

author: @jasonflight3601
text: Good day Robert, Interesting video. I am a geologist new to graphite, but I recently started working on a graphite project. I was wondering what size graphite flakes you were getting on this project? I am under the impression that graphite varies wildly in price ranging from 900$ per tonne to 20,000$ US per tonne for jumbo flake graphite.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Jason Flight This is an old video mate - but you are right price has wide range depending on size, purity and type This is vein graphite so lump size is considerable - up to around 50kg per lump and down to a few microns - but that is fairly typical of Sri Lankan vein graphite. The Turkish open cast stuff is poorer quality and mostly powder whereas kish is about as cheap as you like - where is your project?

replies.author: @WileHeCoyote
replies.text: +Jason Flight   are you working in the graphite creek area of alaska? im looking for pacific north america area graphite contacts. Washington state, Canada, Alaska.

replies.author: @jasonflight3768
replies.text: +MissFit ToyMaker,   I am actually working in Newfoundland Canada, so Atlantic Canada.  Graphite in the area occurs along the base of a large thrust fault.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Jason Flight I am in BC right now but just about to go out to Nova Scotia - in Canadian terms that will make us neighbours lol

replies.author: @jasonflight3768
replies.text:
+Robert Murray-Smith Fire me off an email and I can fill you in on some of the details if you are interested newfoundminerals@gmail.com.   We actually just sold the project primarily as a copper VMS project.   But the agreement required a 43-101 be written on the property so if your interested in graphite that should pop up on SEDAR in the next little while.

Cheers,
Jason

author: @fusiondecor
text:
Hi Robert,
Great vid about mining graphite. I live in Thailand and Thailand doesn't have abundant graphite (at least not enough for mining) I often go into the mountains to look for minerals and metals and I've come across some graphite patches as well. Driven and affected by your enthusiasm about graphite I've revisited one of these patches and brought some ore home. I don't really know what grade the ore is. You can mail me your address so I can send you a sample, sandor_szabo at hotmail.com
